steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage's final sentence gives the answer directly: “The difficulty of determining No. 62's author arises not from a lack of evidence but from too much contradictory evidence.” This is the decisive clue explaining why authorship is difficult to determine.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A is correct: contradictory evidence creates a situation where strong cases exist for either author — exactly what choice A describes.

Choices B, C, and
  • D misread the passage — B invents a detail about separate versions
  • C contradicts the passage by claiming evidence is missing
  • D misconstrues the either-or as collaboration
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
